返回本批次

乌克兰无人机指挥官瞄准了俄罗斯石油、军队和士气

Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights

www.bbc.com 作者 tkgally 分数 50 评论 19 2026/04/28 07:09
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 乌克兰无人机指挥官的目标是俄罗斯的石油、军队和士气。23小时前，莎拉·雷恩斯福德（Sarah Rainsford）南欧和东欧记者，乌克兰东部BBC在乌克兰的秘密地点加入无人机小组。“我们对敌人来说就像一块红布。因为我们正在把战争带到他们的领土上，这样他们也能感受到这一点，”乌克兰士兵说道，同时他的部队正忙着组装远程无人机，准备在俄罗斯发射。像这样的深度袭击持续了数周，特别是针对石油出口设施，这是前所未有的。现在，在一次罕见的采访中，乌克兰所有无人机系统的指挥官告诉BBC，此类袭击将会升级，并声称他的无人机部队还杀死了创纪录数量的士兵，阻碍了俄罗斯沿前线的前进。“俄罗斯领土内1,500至2,000公里（930-1,240英里）不再是‘和平后方’，”罗伯特·布罗夫迪警告说。 “热爱自由的乌克兰‘鸟’无论何时何地都可以飞到那里。”在乌克兰东部的一个毛毛细雨的秘密发射场，远程无人机已做好准备，我们被命令返回安全距离。在俄罗斯军队发现他们并向我们发射弹道导弹之前，该团队迅速展开工作。当第一个装置像一架小型喷气式飞机一样冲向俄罗斯时，伴随着一声喊叫、发动机的巨大转速和一道白色的闪光。总统弗拉基米尔·泽伦斯基称这种深度打击对莫斯科来说“非常痛苦”，尽管最近全球油价飙升，但仍给莫斯科能源部门造成了高达数百亿美元的“严重”损失。此类攻击的增加部分归因于技术。本地生产的无人机变得越来越便宜，飞得更远：我们看到推出的无人机现在可以飞行超过 1,000 公里，其他无人机的飞行距离已经是两倍。但这也与专注有关。除了军事人员和生产之外，俄罗斯的能源出口也被确定为优先目标。BBC/Moose Campbell 乌克兰最近几周一直在加强对俄罗斯的深度打击。英国广播公司(BBC)前往乌克兰东部观看了一次此类无人机的发射，布罗夫迪指挥官表示，“普京提取自然资源，将其转化为血钱，然后以沙希德无人机和弹道导弹的形式直接针对我们。”他为这次袭击辩护。 俄罗斯黑海沿岸图阿普谢的居民抱怨，当地炼油厂几天内遭受第二波重大袭击后，出现了有毒降雨。但布罗夫迪却眼花缭乱。“如果炼油厂是用于战争的赚钱工具，那么它们就是一个合法的军事目标，可能会被摧毁。” 指挥官从地下深处的一个秘密地点在空中发动战争。我们被带到一辆车窗漆黑的面包车里去见他，然后走下楼梯，沿着排列着睡眠舱的走廊，进入一个高科技洞穴，从地板到天花板都布满了屏幕。配乐是一连串的嘟嘟声和叮当声，新鲜的数据被传送给数十名穿着 T 恤和连帽衫、弯腰坐在操纵杆和键盘上的男人。他们正在监控由奇巧和安塔利亚等名字的无人机飞行员直接从战场传输的图像。布罗夫迪的无人系统部队仅占乌克兰军队的 2%，但目前他表示，他们的任务占所有被摧毁目标的三分之一。他告诉我，他们自己的伤亡率已不是什么秘密：每年不到 1%。每次袭击（无论何种形式）都会被拍摄以供验证和记录，墙上的监视器会显示实时更新的详细记分卡。在过去的一周，布罗夫迪报告称，在被占领土以及俄罗斯境内的多个能源设施中，袭击了十几名俄罗斯联邦安全局安全官员。他认为，他的军队对于阻止普京取得任何重大胜利至关重要，特别是他的目标是在几个月内夺取东顿巴斯地区的其他地区。“他在抽什么烟？”布罗夫迪很简短。 “这不现实。这很荒谬。”BBC/Moose Campbell 指挥中心摆满了艺术品，这是对罗伯特·布罗夫迪战前生活的致敬。 四年前，罗伯特·布罗夫迪在佳士得这样的拍卖行里比在肮脏的战壕里更舒服。那时他是一位富裕的粮商，副业是一名艺术品收藏家，他战前生活的片段在地堡周围点缀的乌克兰艺术家的绘画和雕塑中得以保留。它们展示在导弹外壳和捕获的无人机旁边。他是匈牙利人，来自乌克兰西部的乌日霍罗德，最出名的是他的军事呼号“马扎尔”。战前，他把胡子刮得干干净净，现在留着长长的姜黄色和灰色斑点的胡须。这位商人在俄罗斯全面入侵之前报名参加战斗——“我们都知道战争是不可避免的”——最初加入了领土防御，然后经历了一些最激烈的战斗，包括巴赫穆特战役。但正是在此之前，在赫尔松的俄罗斯炮火的压制下，他第一次看到了无人机的潜力。布罗夫迪回忆起他为自己的孩子买的一个设备，开始向他的单位介绍类似的。突然间，他们可以爬到俄罗斯阵地上方，向附近的炮兵队传输实时图像，从而使他们能够发动攻击。他解释说：“这个想法最初是为了自我保护而发展起来的，”但它改变了战场。几个月之内，士兵们就开始制造自己的无人机并附加弹药，很快就以第 414 旅“马扎尔之鸟”而闻名。BBC/Moose Campbell 这架无人机可以飞行 1,000 多公里——其他人可以走得更远。布罗夫迪的战略不仅建立在远程打击的基础上。他详细谈到了另一个优先事项：减少俄罗斯的乌克兰在人力方面具有优势。对于乌克兰来说，这个问题变得更加严重，因为乌克兰正在努力动员人员前往前线：“那些想要战斗的人已经在战斗了，”指挥官承认。因此，他的船员们接到直接命令，每月杀死的敌方士兵数量超过了俄罗斯可以招募的士兵数量。每月有超过 30,000 名人员。“所有无人机袭击中的 30% 必须针对军事人员，”布罗夫迪明确表示。 “你可以称其为杀戮计划，是的，现在我们已经超越了。”他说他们已经连续四个月达到了目标。我无法确认这些数据，但布罗夫迪告诉我他的手下确实是这么做的：每个士兵的死亡都必须通过视频证明，否则就不算数。其中一些片段在指挥中心的屏幕上循环播放，布罗夫迪还在 Telegram 上发布了这些片段，他将自己的无人机部队称为“鸟”，并用俄语来形容他们的无人机部队。他指着我们周围的屏幕说道，“人类历史上对敌人最大规模的屠杀正在这个房间里发生。”这是一个温和的人所说的残酷言论，但布罗夫迪拒绝“被怜悯所折磨”。他说，俄罗斯军队远远超出了他们自己的国界，是由“想要摧毁我们国家”的普京派出的。“如果我们不杀死他们，他们就会杀死我们。那就是明确。”路透社乌克兰的远程无人机袭击了俄罗斯图阿普谢的炼油厂。指挥官坚称他没有“玫瑰色眼镜”：他的目标是遏制，而不是发动新的反攻或夺回大片土地。“我们拥有一种有效的武器：不是发动进攻性战争，而是阻止敌人在我们的领土上有效推进，”他告诉我。他还认为弗拉基米尔·普京无法结束他的军事行动。入侵，因为失败的风险太大。所以布罗夫迪还有一个目标：俄罗斯士气。他希望高伤亡率，再加上边境深处设施的大火，能够在俄罗斯内部造成“某种骚动”。他的目标是令人震惊。最近在乌克兰广泛传播的一段视频显示，图阿普谢的一名俄罗斯妇女泪流满面。 “我只想和我的孩子住在海边，但一切都毁了……那些无人机飞来飞去，摧毁了一切。”她啜泣着，咒骂着。对布罗夫迪来说，这表明俄罗斯的入侵和乌克兰的强烈反击，其后果可能会蔓延到迄今为止有限的范围之外。他的每架无人机的目的都是让更多的俄罗斯人质疑他们国家正在打的战争以及发动这场战争的总统。索菲·威廉姆斯、穆斯·坎贝尔、弗拉基米尔·洛日科和阿纳斯塔西娅·列夫琴科。

Original Summary

  1. Ukraine's drone commander has Russian oil, troops and morale in his sights23 hours agoSarah RainsfordSouthern and Eastern Europe correspondent , Eastern UkraineBBC joins drone team at secret location in Ukraine"We're like a red rag to the enemy. Because we're taking the war to their territory so that they feel it too," the Ukrainian soldier says, as his unit scramble to assemble long-range drones for launch at Russia.Ukraine has been intensifying its deep strikes like this for several weeks, targeting oil export facilities, in particular, like never before.Now, in a rare interview, the commander of all Ukraine's unmanned systems has told the BBC such attacks will escalate and claimed his drone forces are also holding back Russia's advance along the frontline by killing a record number of soldiers."1,500 to 2,000km (930-1,240 miles) inside Russian territory is no longer the 'peaceful rear'," Robert Brovdi warns. "The freedom-loving Ukrainian 'bird' flies there whenever and wherever it wants."At the secret launch site, a drizzly field in eastern Ukraine, the long-range drones are primed and we're ordered back to a safe distance. The team work quickly before Russian forces can detect them and send ballistic missiles hurtling towards us. There's a shouted command, loud revs of an engine and a flash of white as the first device tears into the sky towards Russia like a mini jet plane.President Volodymyr Zelensky calls such deep strikes "very painful" to Moscow, causing "critical" losses running to tens of billions of dollars in its energy sector despite the recent surge in global oil prices.The increase in such attacks is partly down to technology. Locally produced drones are becoming cheaper and flying further: the model we see launch can now travel more than 1,000km and others already go twice as far.But it's also about focus. In addition to military personnel and production, Russia's energy exports have been identified as a priority target.BBC/Moose CampbellUkraine has been intensifying its deep strikes into Russia in recent weeks. The BBC went to see one such drone launch in eastern Ukraine"Putin extracts natural resources and converts them into blood dollars that they then direct against us in the form of Shahed drones and ballistic missiles," says Commander Brovdi, justifying the strikes.Residents in Tuapse on Russia's Black Sea coast complain of toxic rain after a second wave of major strikes on the local refinery in several days. But Brovdi is dry-eyed."If oil refineries are a tool to make money that's used for war, then they are a legitimate military target, subject to destruction."The commander wages war in the skies from a secret location deep underground. We're taken to meet him in a van with blacked out windows, then led down stairs and along corridors lined with sleeping pods to emerge into a high-tech cavern covered in screens from floor to ceiling.The soundtrack is a series of bleeps and pings as fresh data is fed to dozens of men in T-shirts and hoodies hunched over joysticks and keyboards. They're monitoring images streamed directly from the battlefield from drone pilots with names like KitKat and Antalya.Brovdi's Unmanned Systems Forces make up just 2% of Ukraine's military but these days he says they account for a third of all targets destroyed. Their own casualty rate, he tells me, is no secret: less than 1% per year.Each strike – of any kind – is filmed for verification and logged, and monitors on one wall display a detailed scorecard, updated in real time.In the past week, Brovdi has reported hitting a dozen Russian FSB security service officers in occupied territory as well as multiple energy facilities in Russia itself. He argues that his forces are critical to denying Putin any headline victories, especially his aim of seizing the rest of the eastern Donbas region within months."What is he smoking?" Brovdi is curt. "That's not realistic. It's absurd."BBC/Moose CampbellThe command centre is filled with artwork, a nod to Robert Brovdi's life before the warFour years ago, Robert Brovdi was more comfortable in auction houses like Christie's than filthy trenches. A well-off grain dealer in those days, with a sideline as an art collector, fragments of his pre-war life survive in the paintings and sculptures by Ukrainian artists dotted around the bunker. They're displayed beside missile casings and captured drones. He's an ethnic Hungarian, from Uzhhorod in western Ukraine, and best known by his military call sign, Magyar. Clean-shaven before the war, he now wears a long ginger and grey-speckled beard.The businessman signed up to fight just before Russia's full-scale invasion – "we all knew war was inevitable" – initially joining the Territorial Defence, then passing through some of the fiercest battles, including for Bakhmut.But it was before that, pinned down by Russian fire in Kherson, that he first saw the potential of drones. Brovdi recalled a device he'd bought for his own children and began to introduce similar ones to his unit. Suddenly they could climb above Russian positions and stream live images to a nearby artillery team, enabling them to strike. "The idea first developed as self-preservation," he explains, but it transformed the battlefield.Within months the soldiers were building their own drones and attaching munitions, and soon became renowned as 414th Brigade, the Birds of Magyar.BBC/Moose CampbellThis drone can travel over 1,000km - others go furtherBrovdi's strategy is not only built on long-range strikes.He talks, at length, about another priority: reducing Russia's advantage in terms of manpower.The issue has become even more acute for Ukraine as it struggles to mobilise men for the front: "Those who wanted to fight are already fighting," the commander accepts.So his crews are under direct orders to kill more enemy soldiers each month than Russia can recruit. That's over 30,000 men a month."30% of all drone strikes have to be against military personnel," Brovdi is clear. "You can call it a kill plan, yes, and right now we are exceeding it."He says they've met their target for four months in a row.I can't confirm that data, but Brovdi tells me his men do exactly that: the death of each soldier has to be proved by video, or it doesn't count.Some of those clips play on a grim loop on screens in the command centre and Brovdi also posts them on Telegram, where he styles his drone forces as the "birds" and their Russian prey as "worms" to hunt and destroy."The greatest mass killing of an enemy in the history of mankind is taking place in this room," he says at one point, gesturing at the screens around us.It is brutal talk, from a softly spoken man, but Brovdi refuses to be "gnawed by pity".Russian troops are far beyond their own borders, he says, sent by Putin "who wants to destroy our nation"."If we don't kill them, they kill us. That is clear."ReutersUkraine's long-range drones have hit oil refineries such as this one in Tuapse, RussiaThe commander insists he has no "rose-tinted spectacles": his goal is containment, not mounting new counter-offensives or taking back huge swathes of land."We have an effective weapon: not to conduct an offensive war, but to prevent the enemy advancing effectively on our territory," he tells me.He also believes Vladimir Putin cannot afford to end his invasion, because the risks of failure are too great.So Brovdi has one more target: Russian morale.He hopes a high casualty rate, combined with giant fires burning at facilities deep beyond the border, can create "a certain ferment" within Russia. He's aiming for the shock factor.One recent video widely shared in Ukraine shows a Russian woman in Tuapse in floods of tears. "I just wanted to live by the sea with my child, but everything's ruined…those drones fly, destroying everything," she sobs, between expletives.For Brovdi, it's a sign that the fallout from Russia's invasion – and Ukraine's strong pushback – could be spreading beyond its so-far limited circles.His aim, with every drone, is to make more Russians question the war their country is fighting and the president who started it.Additional reporting by Sophie Williams, Moose Campbell, Volodymyr Lozhko and Anastasia Levchenko.

评论（默认中文）

thisislife2 2026/04/28 15:34
啊，西方媒体时不时地宣传“乌克兰正在赢得战争”，让他们的公民处于“好战”的恐慌状态，这样他们就不会反对国防预算的增加。并让欧洲人不再因不求和而感到内疚（ https://www.russiamatters.org/analysis/why-unjust-peace-bett... ）并怂恿乌克兰人继续战斗，尽管乌克兰正在失败并正在系统地去工业化和非军事化（ https://www.russiamatters.org/blog/4-years-later-what-russia... ）。随着欧洲的重新军事化，历史正在重演，看起来我们将经历另一场世界大战。尽管取得了所有进步，欧洲三巨头——英国、法国和俄罗斯（以及新兴大国德国）——仍然拥有卑鄙的帝国主义心态，这导致了这种反复的对抗，将我们所有人拖入他们的泥潭。我真的开始相信美国的说法，即二战后他们在欧盟建立了军事基地，控制了他们所有人。
Ah, the periodic "Ukraine is winning the war" propaganda by western media to keep their citizens in a "warmongering" panic mode so that they don't oppose the hike in defence budgets. And to make the Europeans feel less guilty about not suing for peace ( https://www.russiamatters.org/analysis/why-unjust-peace-bett... ) and egging the Ukrainians to keep fighting even though Ukraine is losing and being systematically deindustrialised and demilitarised ( https://www.russiamatters.org/blog/4-years-later-what-russia... ). History is repeating itself with the re-militarisation of Europe and it looks like we'll experience another World War. For all the progress they have made, the big 3 of Europe - UK, France and Russia (and emerging power Germany) - all still have a shitty imperialist mindsets which leads to this kind of repeated confrontation that drags us all into their muck. I am really beginning to believe the American pitch that they were the one who kept them all in check, after WW2 with their military bases in EU.
在 HN 中查看
tim333 2026/04/28 16:57
我不认为它真的是这样工作的。英国广播公司不会坐视不理——啊，让我们推动一些好战的宣传吧。这更像是俄罗斯的事情。

另外，欧洲人不寻求和平等——这不是欧洲和俄罗斯、北约和俄罗斯、美国和俄罗斯之间的战争。这是俄罗斯对乌克兰的入侵，乌克兰人正在反击。这又是俄罗斯的宣传，说他们对乌克兰的入侵实际上是一场与其他未参与的国家的战争。过去常说美国，但特朗普现在有多少次有效地向乌克兰人大喊投降了？

历史在重演，俄罗斯入侵x国，用一堆废话说这不是它的错等等，这种情况已经发生了大约三十次，最糟糕的是与希特勒合作发动第二次世界大战。

俄罗斯目前的表现并不好。西方媒体一直支持乌克兰，但就连Z宣传者也从俄罗斯不可战胜转向俄罗斯现在正在失败。

这有点像阿富汗的情况。俄罗斯陷入了困境，其政权有望在某个时刻崩溃，就像传统的输掉战争一样。上次我见到普京时，他看上去非常紧张，而且有传言说要发生宫廷政变。

I don't think it really works like that. The bbc doesn't sit around think - ah let's push some warmongering propaganda. That's more a Russian thing.

Also re Europeans not suing for peace etc. - it isn't a war between Europe and Russia or NATO and Russia or the US and Russia. It's an invasion by Russia of Ukraine and the Ukrainians are fighting back. Again that's kind of Russian propaganda to say their invasion of Ukraine is actually a war with someone else not involved. The used to say the US but how many times has Trump effectively yelled surrender at the Ukrainians now?

It's history repeating itself along the Russia invades country x with a bunch of bullshit about how it's not it's fault etc which has happened about thirty times, the worst being partnering with Hitler to start WW2.

Russia isn't doing that well at the moment. The western media has always been pro Ukraine but even the Z propagandists are flipping from Russia is invincible to Russia is losing now.

It's a bit of an Afghanistan like situation. Russia is stuck and its regime will hopefully collapse at some point as seems traditional when it loses wars. Putin was looking very nervous last time I saw him and there are talks of palace coups.

cl0ckt0wer 2026/04/28 07:46
那么他是夸大了还是低估了他们的能力？我很惊讶他们会透露自己的能力。
So is he exaggerating or understating their capabilities? I'm surprised they'd disclose their capabilities.

damidekronik 2026/04/28 07:50
鉴于既定目标，很可能夸大了
Given the stated goal, most likely exaggerating

在 HN 中查看
mschuster91 2026/04/28 07:40
> “俄罗斯领土内 1,500 至 2,000 公里（930-1,240 英里）不再是‘和平后方’，”罗伯特·布罗夫迪警告说。 “热爱自由的乌克兰‘鸟’无论何时何地都可以飞到那里。”

对于美国人来说，是纽约到堪萨斯城。对于欧洲人来说，从哥本哈根到罗马。

乌克兰人能够在这么远的距离上对无人机进行指挥和控制，这真是太疯狂了。可能大部分是通过星链……

> "1,500 to 2,000km (930-1,240 miles) inside Russian territory is no longer the 'peaceful rear'," Robert Brovdi warns. "The freedom-loving Ukrainian 'bird' flies there whenever and wherever it wants."

For comparison for the Americans, it's New York to Kansas City. For the Europeans, Copenhagen to Rome.

Crazy that the Ukrainians can keep up command-and-control for drones over such a distance. Probably most of it is via Starlink...

在 HN 中查看
tim333 2026/04/28 17:12
还有其他技术。 GPS 是一个明显的GPS，尽管它可以被屏蔽。我认为 HIMAR 使用 GPS 并在丢失时进行惯性备份。此外，通过现代图像识别，您可以为物体提供最后一点应该击中的物体的图片。我不确定使用了哪些实际技术。乌克兰建立了一个市场，战斗机可以从国防初创公司订购所需的技术。与俄罗斯相比，这是一个显着的优势，俄罗斯拥有类似的技术，但在如何分配方面存在官僚主义和腐败。
There are other technologies. GPS is an obvious one though it can be blocked. I think HIMARs used GPS with inertial backup if it's lost. Also with modern image recognition you can give the thing a picture of what it's supposed to hit for the last bit. I'm not sure which actual techs are used. Ukraine has a market set up where fighters can order the tech they need from defence startups. It's a significant advantage over Russia which has comparable tech but is bureaucratic and corrupt as to how it's allocated.
在 HN 中查看
icegreentea2 2026/04/28 09:42
乌克兰的“远程攻击无人机”实际上只是成本优化的巡航导弹。对战略目标进行远程打击无需人为控制。例如，最初的“战斧”可以进行地形雷达匹配（+惯性制导）来进行导航，并拥有一个完全基于机载摄像头的系统来进行终端制导。
Ukraine's "long range attack drones" are really just cost optimized cruise missiles. Long-range strike against strategic targets is something you can handle without man in loop control. For example, the original Tomahawks could do terrain radar matching (+inertial guidance) for navigation, and had a fully onboard camera based system for terminal guidance.
在 HN 中查看
cyberge99 2026/04/28 11:00
您实际上可以将无人机作为星链天线+rpi 组合来驾驶。 让无人机飞得很远，然后在规定的时间之前保持无线电静默。 这样做几次，您就可以相对轻松地获得 CNC 加工。
You could literally fly drone as a starlink antenna+rpi combo. Have that drone fly far in and then go radio silent until a prescribed time. Do this a few times and you could get CNC pretty far in relatively lightly.
在 HN 中查看
stavros 2026/04/28 09:22
我不知道，ELRS/LoRa 非常神奇。我不知道那里会发生什么样的干扰，但只要有足够大（且高）的天线，你应该能够到达很远的地方。
I don't know, ELRS/LoRa is pretty amazing. I don't know what kind of jamming happens there, but with a big (and tall) enough antenna, you should be able to get pretty far.
在 HN 中查看
cyberge99 2026/04/28 11:01
这也是我的想法。 与无人机上的 Starlink 结合使用，您可以深入探索。
This was my thought too. Couple with Starlink on a drone and you could go deep.

outside1234 2026/04/28 07:49

Ukraine is going to win this war if we can just keep funding them long enough
在 HN 中查看
matusp 2026/04/28 15:29
他们会输，问题是输多少。我不认为俄罗斯人会失去他们现在占领的领土，除非发生一些黑天鹅事件。俄罗斯还能征服多少领土，直到他们失去胃口，还有待观察。
They are going to lose, the question is how much. I don't see Russians losing the territory they occupy right now, unless some black swan event happens. It remains to be seen how much more territory Russia is able to conquer until they lose their appetite.
在 HN 中查看
JohnnyLarue 2026/04/28 12:26
他们几年前就应该投降，现在他们将面临同样的领土损失，而且还会有数十万人死亡。
They should have surrendered years ago, now they're going to wind up with the same territory losses and a few hundred thousand dead on top of it.
在 HN 中查看
tim333 2026/04/28 17:17
你见过俄罗斯人如何用酷刑室对待被征服的领土并不得不终身服从独裁统治吗？生命往往只有几个月，因为他们将男性围捕起来，强迫他们加入俄罗斯军队并被杀害。
Have you seen how the Russians have treated conquered territories with torture chambers and having to submit to the dictatorship for life? And life is often only a few months as they round the males up and force them into the Russian army to get killed.

drysine 2026/04/28 13:48

And to replenish their losses Ukrainian regime snatches men on the street. There are thousands of videos made by bystanders.[0] Come and see.

P.S. I love the ad I see on that site: "Over 10 million Ukrainians suffer from anxiety due to the war. Free exercises with scientifically proven effectiveness." The most important exercise now is running - it saves your life.
